title: "God's Grace Is Sufficient - Part Two" II Corinthians 12:9

notes.introduction: A closing message in a year-long series through 2 Corinthians, anchored in 2 Corinthians 12:9, presenting a third point to a sermon preached one year earlier. Draws on Acts 14, 16, 23, and 25 and 2 Timothy 4 to illustrate the Apostle Paul experiencing the sufficiency of God's grace across repeated trials, stonings, imprisonments, and assassination plots.

sections.heading: Review of Part One: Promise Explained and Embraced
Acts 23: Conspiracy to Kill Paul in Jerusalem
Acts 25 and 2 Timothy 4: Further Deliverances and Paul's Summary
Application to the Congregation and Transition to 2026
